chunks.text: If I had sent the letter that day, everything might have been different.
My childhood friend had moved to a distant town.
I had only to tell her a single " thank you.
" A single letter would have been enough.
If I had had a little more courage at the time
the letter would have reached her, and she might have remembered me for longer.
But I did not put it into words.
I had hidden my words away, treasuring them too carefully.
When I realized it, I no longer knew the address of her town.
If I had written that one letter
I might have been corresponding with her to this day.
Long after, when I heard the news of her marriage
I tasted that small regret once more.
I learned that life can change upon the courage of a single letter.
